description: The following recommendations should be considered while configuring the IP addresses for primary and backup controllers: While configuring a primary-controller ip <ipv4-address>, it is recommended to use the IP address of a standalone controller or an IP address of a controller in the primary cluster. Similarly, while configuring a backup-controller ip <ipv4-address>, it is recommended to use the IP address of a standalone controller or an IP address of a controller in the backup cluster. Configuring both primary or backup controller IP addresses from the same cluster is not supported.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.13.1130, what caveat is documented for User Based Tunnel?

Feature Caveat: User Based Tunnel - The following recommendations should be considered while configuring the IP addresses for primary and backup controllers: While configuring a primary-controller ip <ipv4-address>, it is recommended to use the IP address of a standalone controller or an IP address of a controller in the primary cluster. Similarly, while configuring a backup-controller ip <ipv4-address>, it is recommended to use the IP address of a standalone controller or an IP address of a controller in the backup cluster. Configuring both primary or backup controller IP addresses from the same cluster is not supported.

description: User-Based Tunneling (UBT) and DHCPv4/v6 snooping are mutually exclusive features in AOS-CX. When these two features are configured simultaneously, binding entries for the end clients will not be populated in the binding database. IP source-lockdown feature permits traffic from the clients to pass through the switch only if the client's MAC and IP addresses are present in the binding database. IP source lockdown can be enabled at both port and VLAN levels. The port-level configuration will be overridden by the VLAN-level configuration only when the port is a member of a single VLAN. If the port is a member of multiple VLANs, then the VLAN-level configuration for IP source lockdown will not be applied. Client IP tracker can track the IP address until the IP source lockdown is applied. Once the lockdown is in effect, the IP address will only be tracked if there is a valid entry in the binding table. However, if an IP address is already being tracked, it will remain active through probes, as probes utilize ARP packets, which are Layer 2 (L2) packets rather than Layer 3 (L3) packets.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.13.1130, what caveat is documented for Client IP Tracker?

Feature Caveat: Client IP Tracker - User-Based Tunneling (UBT) and DHCPv4/v6 snooping are mutually exclusive features in AOS-CX. When these two features are configured simultaneously, binding entries for the end clients will not be populated in the binding database. IP source-lockdown feature permits traffic from the clients to pass through the switch only if the client's MAC and IP addresses are present in the binding database. IP source lockdown can be enabled at both port and VLAN levels. The port-level configuration will be overridden by the VLAN-level configuration only when the port is a member of a single VLAN. If the port is a member of multiple VLANs, then the VLAN-level configuration for IP source lockdown will not be applied. Client IP tracker can track the IP address until the IP source lockdown is applied. Once the lockdown is in effect, the IP address will only be tracked if there is a valid entry in the binding table. However, if an IP address is already being tracked, it will remain active through probes, as probes utilize ARP packets, which are Layer 2 (L2) packets rather than Layer 3 (L3) packets.

description: The switch does not support double encapsulation. A packet can be encapsulated with either L2GRE (UBT) or VXLAN, but not both. The network administrator should decide the tunneling type to be used, and then plan the configuration accordingly.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.13.1130, what limitation is mentioned for User Based Tunnel?

Feature Caveat: User Based Tunnel - The switch does not support double encapsulation. A packet can be encapsulated with either L2GRE (UBT) or VXLAN, but not both. The network administrator should decide the tunneling type to be used, and then plan the configuration accordingly.

description: In the event of license issues when onboarding a DUT to primary or backup mobility conductor, the DUT will not try to bootstrap to another mobility conductor where a license is available. For example, if the primary mobility conductor does not have a license to onboard the DUT but the backup mobility conductor does have adequate licenses, if both mobility conductors are reachable then UBT will be down, and the DUT will not attempt to bootstrap to the backup controller. However, if the primary mobility conductor is not reachable, the DUT gets tunneled to the standby/backup mobility conductor. Once the primary mobility conductor is reachable by the DUT once again, the DUT will not automatically bootstrap back to the primary. Network administrators should manually disable and enable UBT on the DUT to re-establish the tunnel to the primary mobility conductor.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.13.1130, what additional caveat is documented for User Based Tunnel?

Feature Caveat: User Based Tunnel - In the event of license issues when onboarding a DUT to primary or backup mobility conductor, the DUT will not try to bootstrap to another mobility conductor where a license is available. For example, if the primary mobility conductor does not have a license to onboard the DUT but the backup mobility conductor does have adequate licenses, if both mobility conductors are reachable then UBT will be down, and the DUT will not attempt to bootstrap to the backup controller. However, if the primary mobility conductor is not reachable, the DUT gets tunneled to the standby/backup mobility conductor. Once the primary mobility conductor is reachable by the DUT once again, the DUT will not automatically bootstrap back to the primary. Network administrators should manually disable and enable UBT on the DUT to re-establish the tunnel to the primary mobility conductor.

description: When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. If the Aruba Central feature is enabled using this command, the switch will then reconnect back to Aruba Central and will get disconnected again. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.13.1130, what caveat is documented for Central?

Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. If the Aruba Central feature is enabled using this command, the switch will then reconnect back to Aruba Central and will get disconnected again. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.

description: When a hot-patch file download is triggered using the switch WebUI, log messages can incorrectly state that the file is added to the database with a missing status. This is a temporary state, and will correctly change to Not applied once the download is completed.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.13.1130, what caveat is documented for Hot Patch?

Feature Caveat: Hot Patch - When a hot-patch file download is triggered using the switch WebUI, log messages can incorrectly state that the file is added to the database with a missing status. This is a temporary state, and will correctly change to Not applied once the download is completed.

description: Only static VXLAN is supported.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.13.1130, what caveat is documented for VXLAN?
Feature Caveat: VXLAN - Only static VXLAN is supported.

description: The R8V13A 6200F Switch does not support mixed VSF stacking.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.13.1130, what caveat is documented for VSF?
Feature Caveat: VSF - The R8V13A 6200F Switch does not support mixed VSF stacking.

description: When SNMP is enabled via the switch CLI, it can take between 1-2 minutes for the SNMP daemon to be ready to respond to requests. If a local or external SNMP MIB walk is performed in the interval between when SNMP is first enabled and the SNMP daemon is ready, the MIB walk action will return an error.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.13.1130, what caveat is documented for SNMP?

Feature Caveat: SNMP - When SNMP is enabled via the switch CLI, it can take between 1-2 minutes for the SNMP daemon to be ready to respond to requests. If a local or external SNMP MIB walk is performed in the interval between when SNMP is first enabled and the SNMP daemon is ready, the MIB walk action will return an error.

description: When a switch uses a certificate with a legacy certificate name that is not supported in 10.12 because it contains disallowed characters, the information will migrate properly in the upgrade, but that certificate can no longer be edited. For new certificate names, only alphanumeric characters, dots, dashes, and underscores are allowed.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.13.1130, what caveat is documented for Certificates?

Feature Caveat: Certificates - When a switch uses a certificate with a legacy certificate name that is not supported in 10.12 because it contains disallowed characters, the information will migrate properly in the upgrade, but that certificate can no longer be edited. For new certificate names, only alphanumeric characters, dots, dashes, and underscores are allowed.

description: NOTE In a VSF stack, the switch may fail to log events for the matching access-list entries. ACL functionality is not impacted; access-list entries are applied properly and only the logging is incorrectly generated.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.13.1130, what caveat is documented for ACLs?

Feature Caveat: ACLs - NOTE In a VSF stack, the switch may fail to log events for the matching access-list entries. ACL functionality is not impacted; access-list entries are applied properly and only the logging is incorrectly generated.

description: VSF stack formation is blocked when there are reserved autojoin interfaces (25, 26, 49, 50) in the stack topology.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.13.1130, what caveat is documented for Aruba CX Mobile App?
Feature Caveat: Aruba CX Mobile App - VSF stack formation is blocked when there are reserved autojoin interfaces (25, 26, 49, 50) in the stack topology.

description: For Classifier policy modifications to be secure, Aruba strongly encourages modifications be done as a three-step process: Bring down the port, modify, and bring the port back up.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.13.1130, what caveat is documented for Classifiers?

Feature Caveat: Classifiers - For Classifier policy modifications to be secure, Aruba strongly encourages modifications be done as a three-step process: Bring down the port, modify, and bring the port back up.

description: Policies containing both MAC and IPv6 classes are not allowed.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.13.1130, what limitation is mentioned for Classifiers?
Feature Caveat: Classifiers - Policies containing both MAC and IPv6 classes are not allowed.

description: No other checkpoint besides "startup-configuration" gets migrated during the upgrade process.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.13.1130, what caveat is documented for CMF?
Feature Caveat: CMF - No other checkpoint besides "startup-configuration" gets migrated during the upgrade process.

feature: DHCP Server, DHCP Relay, and DHCP Snooping
description: DHCP Relay and DHCP Snooping can co-exist on the same switch. DHCP Snooping and DHCP Server cannot co-exist on the same switch. DHCP Snooping, DHCP Relay, and DHCP Server together cannot co-exist on the same switch.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.13.1130, what caveat is documented for DHCP Server, DHCP Relay, and DHCP Snooping?

Feature Caveat: DHCP Server, DHCP Relay, and DHCP Snooping - DHCP Relay and DHCP Snooping can co-exist on the same switch. DHCP Snooping and DHCP Server cannot co-exist on the same switch. DHCP Snooping, DHCP Relay, and DHCP Server together cannot co-exist on the same switch.

description: Reserved ports or ports used by other applications/services within the system are not recommended to be used for other services. When two services use the same port there is chance of unexpected behaviors from these services. Best practice is to use unique port for each service across system.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.13.1130, what caveat is documented for IP-SLA?

Feature Caveat: IP-SLA - Reserved ports or ports used by other applications/services within the system are not recommended to be used for other services. When two services use the same port there is chance of unexpected behaviors from these services. Best practice is to use unique port for each service across system.

description: The switch may only software forward at a rate of 100pps if the packets that trigger ICMP redirect.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.13.1130, what caveat is documented for ICMP Redirect?
Feature Caveat: ICMP Redirect - The switch may only software forward at a rate of 100pps if the packets that trigger ICMP redirect.

description: MACsec works between a CX device and a Windows VM running AnyConnect with AES-128 cipher. AnyConnect does not support AES-256 in the NAM module (works only for the VPN module).

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.13.1130, what caveat is documented for MACsec?

Feature Caveat: MACsec - MACsec works between a CX device and a Windows VM running AnyConnect with AES-128 cipher. AnyConnect does not support AES-256 in the NAM module (works only for the VPN module).

description: When Cisco AnyConnect is used as dot1x supplicant, it is recommended to configure cak-length to be 16 under dot1x-authenticator mode.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.13.1130, what limitation is mentioned for MACsec?
Feature Caveat: MACsec - When Cisco AnyConnect is used as dot1x supplicant, it is recommended to configure cak-length to be 16 under dot1x-authenticator mode.

description: Ensure the cipher suite GCM-AES-128 is configured when AOS-CX is acting as a key server. This is because, by default AOS-CX will use the most secure cipher suite gcm-aes-xpn-256 for establishing MACsec secure link and Comware/PVOS doesn’t support an XPN cipher suite.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.13.1130, what additional caveat is documented for MACsec?

Feature Caveat: MACsec - Ensure the cipher suite GCM-AES-128 is configured when AOS-CX is acting as a key server. This is because, by default AOS-CX will use the most secure cipher suite gcm-aes-xpn-256 for establishing MACsec secure link and Comware/PVOS doesn’t support an XPN cipher suite.

description: OSPFv2 and OSPFv3 do not support detailed LSA show commands.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.13.1130, what caveat is documented for OSPF?
Feature Caveat: OSPF - OSPFv2 and OSPFv3 do not support detailed LSA show commands.

description: Authorization by means of HPE VSAs is not supported.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.13.1130, what caveat is documented for RADIUS?
Feature Caveat: RADIUS - Authorization by means of HPE VSAs is not supported.

description: REST supports the 'admin' and 'operator' roles but does not work with TACACS+ command authorization.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.13.1130, what caveat is documented for REST?
Feature Caveat: REST - REST supports the 'admin' and 'operator' roles but does not work with TACACS+ command authorization.

description: RIP/RIPng metric configuration support is not available.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.13.1130, what caveat is documented for RIP/RIPng?
Feature Caveat: RIP/RIPng - RIP/RIPng metric configuration support is not available.

description: UBT VLAN local mode does not support multiple MAC addresses or clients in same VLAN on the same switch port, as this configuration can cause mac-move issues on an L2 switch connected to a UBT Switch. Use UBT vlan-extend mode to support multiple MAC address or clients in a differently tagged VLAN on a single switchport.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.13.1130, what caveat is documented for UBT Design?

Feature Caveat: UBT Design - UBT VLAN local mode does not support multiple MAC addresses or clients in same VLAN on the same switch port, as this configuration can cause mac-move issues on an L2 switch connected to a UBT Switch. Use UBT vlan-extend mode to support multiple MAC address or clients in a differently tagged VLAN on a single switchport.

description: Symptom: When wireless clients roam between access points connected to the same switch, they lose connectivity and receive an APIPA address. Connectivity is only restored after the MAC-age timer expires. Scenario: When a client is authenticated with a VLAN on a secure port, the VLAN is added as an ingress-only VLAN on other secure ports, but the MSTP state for those ports remains in blocked state instead of forwarding state, preventing seamless client movement. Workaround: Reduce the MAC-age timer to minimize the impact. or configure AP ports in "device-mode" instead of "client-mode":{color:#000000}interface 1/1/X port-access client-limit 1 port-access client-mode device{color}

description: Symptom: Missing ARP entries showing as "incomplete" in the ARP table, causing connectivity issues in remote VTEP. Scenario: This issue may occur in an EVPN VXLAN environment with VSX pairs when a VIP (Virtual IP) moves from one node to another node, MAC address aging occurs or after executing "clear mac-address" on the switch. Workaround: A temporary workaround is to add a static ARP entry for the affected IP addresses.

workaround: is to add a static ARP entry for the affected IP addresses.

description: Symptom: Excessive connection lost and connection established logging when multiple NTP servers are configured. Scenario: If the NTP server changes, a connection lost and connection established is logged to the event log. Workaround: The fix disables logging of normal server changes and only logs events if server reachability reaches 0 or NTP synchronization is lost.
symptom: Excessive connection lost and connection established logging when multiple NTP servers are configured.
scenario: If the NTP server changes, a connection lost and connection established is logged to the event log.
workaround: The fix disables logging of normal server changes and only logs events if server reachability reaches 0 or NTP synchronization is lost.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.13.1130, what issue was resolved in NTP Bug 371717?

NTP (Bug ID 371717): Symptom: Excessive connection lost and connection established logging when multiple NTP servers are configured. Scenario: If the NTP server changes, a connection lost and connection established is logged to the event log. Workaround: The fix disables logging of normal server changes and only logs events if server reachability reaches 0 or NTP synchronization is lost.

description: Symptom: Switch-Transceiver is in down state with reason "Module interface error.". Scenario: This issue occurs when flow control is enabled on a particular interface and bi-directional traffic is sent at line rate of 99%. When a shut and no shut (link up down) is done at a particular interface module, interface error is seen. Workaround: Call pv_network_ports_enable(node_id, _port, False) after disabling TM using pv_tm_port_disable.

description: Symptom: Traffic loss in an SVI with vsx active-forwarding enabled, when vsx active-forwarding is disabled/removed on a entirely different SVI. Scenario: In a configuration where vsx active-forwarding is enabled, a special HW resource must be programmed for the VSX peers to handle traffic destined to the active-gateway IP. The trigger for this programming is tied to the router MAC programming), and the ref counting for these router MACs would decrement early, removing the special HW resource and causing traffic loss to occur depending on which peer it lands on. Workaround: There are two possible workarounds: 1. Unconfigure and reconfigure vsx active-forwarding in the SVI. 2. Save the configuration and reboot the VSX peers
symptom: Traffic loss in an SVI with vsx active-forwarding enabled, when vsx active-forwarding is disabled/removed on a entirely different SVI.
scenario: In a configuration where vsx active-forwarding is enabled, a special HW resource must be programmed for the VSX peers to handle traffic destined to the active-gateway IP. The trigger for this programming is tied to the router MAC programming), and the ref counting for these router MACs would decrement early, removing the special HW resource and causing traffic loss to occur depending on which peer it lands on.
workaround: s: 1. Unconfigure and reconfigure vsx active-forwarding in the SVI. 2. Save the configuration and reboot the VSX peers

description: Symptom: System reboots on VSF members with the a reboot reason of "critical service fault." The stack expriences a reboot dut to the failure of stack members to exchange heartbeat messages. Scenario: This issue occurs on the VSF stack device on the 6200 V1 platform switches. Workaround: Check memory allocations done for Q7 on the VSF link port.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.13.1130, what is the workaround for Thermal Manager Bug 359193?
Thermal Manager (Bug ID 359193): The documented workaround is: Update to a different code version.

section: Manual Configuration Restore for Software Downgrade
task: Manual Configuration Restore for Software Downgrade
procedure: Use the show checkpoint command to see the saved checkpoints and ensure that you have a checkpoint that is an exact match of the target software version (see the Image Version column in the output of the command, for example, ML.10.xx.yyyy).
Copy the backup checkpoint into the startup-config.
Boot the switch to the target version (lower version), making sure to select no when prompted to save the current configuration.
description: Use the show checkpoint command to see the saved checkpoints and ensure that you have a checkpoint that is an exact match of the target software version (see the Image Version column in the output of the command, for example, ML.10.xx.yyyy). Copy the backup checkpoint into the startup-config. Boot the switch to the target version (lower version), making sure to select no when prompted to save the current configuration.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.13.1130, how do you restore configuration after a software downgrade?

Downgrade Restore: To restore configuration after a software downgrade, follow the documented procedure in order: 1. Use the show checkpoint command to see the saved checkpoints and ensure that you have a checkpoint that is an exact match of the target software version (see the Image Version column in the output of the command, for example, ML.10.xx.yyyy). 2. Copy the backup checkpoint into the startup-config. 3. Boot the switch to the target version (lower version), making sure to select no when prompted to save the current configuration.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.13.1130, what is the manual configuration restore procedure for software downgrade?

Downgrade Restore: To restore configuration after a software downgrade, follow the documented procedure in order: 1. Use the show checkpoint command to see the saved checkpoints and ensure that you have a checkpoint that is an exact match of the target software version (see the Image Version column in the output of the command, for example, ML.10.xx.yyyy). 2. Copy the backup checkpoint into the startup-config. 3. Boot the switch to the target version (lower version), making sure to select no when prompted to save the current configuration.

procedure: Copy the new image into the non-current boot bank on the switch using your preferred method.

Depending on the version being updated, there may be device component updates needed. Preview any devices updates needed using the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command and entering n when asked to continue.

When ready to update the system, if a non-failsafe device update is needed, make sure the system will not have any power interruption during the process. Invoke the allow unsafe updates command to allow updates to proceed after a switch reboot. Proceed to step 4 within the configured time.
Use the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command to initiate the upgrade. On the switch console port, output similar to the documented example will be displayed as various components are being updated.
description: Copy the new image into the non-current boot bank on the switch using your preferred method. Depending on the version being updated, there may be device component updates needed. Preview any devices updates needed using the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command and entering n when asked to continue. When ready to update the system, if a non-failsafe device update is needed, make sure the system will not have any power interruption during the process. Invoke the allow unsafe updates command to allow updates to proceed after a switch reboot. Proceed to step 4 within the configured time. Use the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command to initiate the upgrade. On the switch console port, output similar to the documented example will be displayed as various components are being updated.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.13.1130, how do you perform the software upgrade?

Upgrade Procedure: To perform the software upgrade, follow the documented procedure in order: 1. Copy the new image into the non-current boot bank on the switch using your preferred method. 2. Depending on the version being updated, there may be device component updates needed. Preview any devices updates needed using the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command and entering n when asked to continue. 3. When ready to update the system, if a non-failsafe device update is needed, make sure the system will not have any power interruption during the process. Invoke the allow unsafe updates command to allow updates to proceed after a switch reboot. Proceed to step 4 within the configured time. 4. Use the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command to initiate the upgrade. On the switch console port, output similar to the documented example will be displayed as various components are being updated.
